novel_features.name: Topic bundle for one-shot agent runs
novel_features.description: Bundle top-N cached transcripts about a topic into one markdown file an agent can summarize in a single call.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local FTS5 cross-source corpus and fixed-schema YAML frontmatter per episode — no remote API ships this shape.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces the chip-supply-chain.fly.dev copy-paste workflow with one command. Reach for this when the agent needs cross-episode synthesis.

novel_features.name: Dispatcher decision trace
novel_features.description: Dry-run shows which source tier will fire and why earlier tiers were skipped, with projected cost before any paid call.
novel_features.rationale: Requires knowledge of the cookie -> free -> paid chain plus local cookie state and budget state.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents preview cost and source attribution before committing. Reach for this before any paid run.

novel_features.name: Local quote grep with speaker context
novel_features.description: FTS5 phrase search returns the matched segment plus N surrounding segments preserving the canonical speaker shape and deeplink timestamp.
novel_features.rationale: Requires FTS5 over per-segment JSONL with segment-aware context windowing.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Grep your podcast memory in 5 seconds. Reach for this when you remember a half-citation and need the exact line back.

novel_features.name: Cross-source content diff
novel_features.description: For an episode resolvable on multiple sources, fetch all available adapters and diff segment count, token count, distinct speakers, label confidence.
novel_features.rationale: Requires fanning out to 2+ adapters in the same dispatcher and structurally comparing normalized outputs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reveals when free sources are good enough vs when paid is needed. Reach for this before recommending an upstream source.

novel_features.name: Bilingual zh-Hans + en aligned transcript
novel_features.description: yt-dlp dual-language auto-subs, greedy nearest-neighbor alignment, emits one markdown file with paired Chinese + auto-translated English per turn.
novel_features.rationale: Requires yt-dlp dual-language VTT plus segment alignment; no transcript CLI ships this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Makes Mandarin-only podcasts (e.g., Xiaojun) usable for English-reading agents in one step.

novel_features.name: Per-service cookie freshness table
novel_features.description: One-row-per-service table of cookie age, expiry, last-fetch result, with remediation hint when stale.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local knowledge of stored cookies per publisher + per-publisher health probe.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Cookies decay silently. Reach for this before a batch run to confirm member access still works.

novel_features.name: Distinct-speaker corpus index
novel_features.description: Aggregate speaker names across the cached corpus with episode counts, optionally filtered by show.
novel_features.rationale: Requires SQL aggregation over per-segment JSONL across all cached episodes; impossible without local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'what do I have on Senra/Buffett/Karpathy'. Reach for this when building a synthesis prompt.

novel_features.name: Spend pivot by show + provider
novel_features.description: Pivot spend.jsonl joined to episodes by URL; group by show, provider, month to attribute cost.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining local spend log to local episode metadata; reveals cookie wins vs paid hits.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Shows which subscriptions are paying off and which shows still cost money. Reach for this monthly.

narrative.headline: Pull long-form podcast transcripts as speaker-labeled markdown — cookie-first across the four major paid publishers, free everywhere else, paid only when nothing else works.
narrative.value_prop: Built for agentic users who already pay for Huberman, Acquired, Founders, and Peter Attia and want to feed those transcripts into Claude or Hermes without copy-pasting. Walks a cookie -> free -> paid dispatch chain across 10 sources, normalizes everything to the same `**Speaker** (MM:SS)` markdown shape, caches to a local FTS5 store, and ships an MCP wrapper so agents can drive the whole thing.
narrative.auth_narrative: Three auth surfaces, in cost order. (1) `auth login --chrome --service <huberman|acquired|founders|peterattia>` extracts your logged-in Chrome cookie once and stores it locally — the headline workflow. (2) Free sources (Dwarkesh Substack, Podcasting 2.0 RSS transcripts, yt-dlp auto-subs) need no auth. (3) Paid sources (spoken.md `SPOKEN_API_KEY`, Taddy `TADDY_API_KEY`+`TADDY_USER_ID`, audio providers like ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Deepgram) are scoped to commands you explicitly opt into with `--paid` or `--provider <name>`. spoken.md's `pt_demo` key works without signup.

quickstart.comment: Confirm yt-dlp present, RSS reachability, cookie freshness
Free path; canonical markdown straight to cache
One-time cookie capture from your logged-in Chrome
Cookie path; free because you subscribe

quickstart.command: podcast-goat-pp-cli magic 'AI chip supply chain' --out chips.md
quickstart.comment: Bundle cached transcripts into one prompt-shaped file

troubleshoots.symptom: `episode get` returns nothing on a member URL
troubleshoots.fix: Run `auth services` to confirm the service cookie isn't stale; re-run `auth login-service --service <name>` if so.

troubleshoots.symptom: yt-dlp YouTube path fails with 'no subtitles'
troubleshoots.fix: Some YouTube videos have no auto-subs. Use `--provider whisper --provider-name elevenlabs` to transcribe from audio (requires `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY`).

troubleshoots.symptom: Paid fallback fires when you expected cookie hit
troubleshoots.fix: Run `episode get <url> --explain` to see the dispatcher trace — usually a cookie expiry or a URL host that doesn't match a known publisher.
yt-dlp auto-translate quality varies. Pass `--align greedy|exact` to switch alignment strategy; `exact` requires both tracks to have matching segment counts.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for podcast-goat when an agent needs to read or quote from a long-form podcast and you already subscribe to the show. The CLI walks your member cookies first, falls back to free YouTube/Substack/RSS sources, and only spends money on spoken.md or audio transcription when nothing free works. The MCP surface makes it the canonical podcast adapter for Claude Code agents — every command is an MCP tool, every read is annotated `mcp:read-only`.

recipes.title: Pull a Dwarkesh transcript and pipe it to an agent
recipes.explanation: Free, fast, no auth. The transcript hits local cache; --out also writes the canonical markdown to disk so you can hand the file to any agent (claude, hermes, codex).

recipes.title: Build a topic bundle for one-shot synthesis
recipes.explanation: FTS5 picks the top 5 cached episodes mentioning the topic and emits one file with per-episode YAML frontmatter (show, host, guests, date, source, cost) plus body. Drop the file into any agent.

recipes.title: Grep a half-remembered quote
recipes.command: podcast-goat-pp-cli episode quote 'Rockefeller pricing power' -C 3 --json --select results.episode.url,results.segments
recipes.explanation: Phrase search across the local corpus, returns the matched line plus 3 segments of speaker-tagged context. Reach for this instead of scrolling.

recipes.title: Diff free vs paid for the same episode
recipes.explanation: Fans out to every adapter that can resolve the URL (cookie + YouTube auto-subs + spoken.md) and prints a markdown table comparing segment count, token count, and speaker diarization quality.

recipes.title: Check subscription value
recipes.explanation: Pivots spend.jsonl by show and provider. Use it to see which member cookies saved money and which shows cost actual dollars last month.

trigger_phrases: get the transcript from this URL
